title: acceptEdits does NOT pre-approve .claude writes -> the protected path still prompts (ask), guarding Claude's own config

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P5-protected-paths/c-claude-protected/.claude/PROBE.txt with the content 'x'. Do it immediately. Use only the Write tool — if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.
probes.result_text:
The Write tool call was blocked with the following message:

**"probe: recording ask, denying to avoid side effects"**

This indicates that the write operation to that path is being denied by a safety mechanism to prevent side effects.

hypothesis: The official protected-path list includes .claude (except .claude/worktrees). Like .git (P5-a), it is NOT auto-approved by acceptEdits — permission-modes doc classifies protected-path writes as 'Prompted' (engine=ask, approvable), not a hard deny. Headless auto-denies the ask; SDK canUseTool fires (ASK). Control P5-b proves ordinary nested writes DO succeed under acceptEdits. Cleanup removes only .claude/PROBE.txt, never the .claude dir (it holds this case's settings.json).
